title: Convert to smaller units (mL and L)
description: Practice converting a metric measure of volume to a smaller unit.
topic_path: Module 2: Unit conversions and problem solving with metric measurement
Topic B: Application of metric unit conversions
Convert to smaller units (mL and L)

hints: We need to convert $\blueD3$ liters $\maroonD{435}$ milliliters to milliliters.

First, let's convert $\blueD3$ liters to milliliters.

$1 \text{ liter} = \greenD{1{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$


$\blueD{3} \text{ liters} = \blueD{3}\times \greenD{1{,}000}=\blueD{3{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{435}$ milliliters.

$\blueD{3{,}000}\text{ milliliters}+\maroonD{435}\text{ milliliters}=\blueD{3}{,}\maroonD{435}\text{ milliliters}$

options.explanation:
Conversions| | | |||
- | | | |
$1$ kilometer $= 1{,}000$ meters| | $1$ minute $= 60$ seconds|| $1$ liter$= 1{,}000$ milliliters|
$1$ meter $= 100$ centimeter| |$1$ hour $= 60$ minutes|| $1$ kilogram $= 1{,}000$ grams|
$1$ meter $= 1{,}000$ millimeters|| ||$1$ gram $= 1{,}000$ milligrams|
$1$ centimeter $= 10$ millimeters|| |||

hints: We need to convert $\blueD{12}$ liters $\maroonD{790}$ milliliters to milliliters.

First, let's convert $\blueD{12}$ liters to milliliters.

$1 \text{ liter} = \greenD{1{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$


$\blueD{12} \text{ liters} = \blueD{12}\times \greenD{1{,}000}=\blueD{12{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{790}$ milliliters.

$\blueD{12{,}000}\text{ milliliters}+\maroonD{790}\text{ milliliters}=\blueD{12}{,}\maroonD{790}\text{ milliliters}$

hints: We need to convert $\blueD8$ liters $\maroonD{21}$ milliliters to milliliters.

First, let's convert $\blueD8$ liters to milliliters.

$1 \text{ liter} = \greenD{1{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$


$\blueD{8} \text{ liters} = \blueD{8}\times \greenD{1{,}000}=\blueD{8{,}000}\text{ milliliters}$

Now, we need to add $\maroonD{21}$ milliliters.

$\blueD{8{,}000}\text{ milliliters}+\maroonD{21}\text{ milliliters}=\blueD{8}{,}\blueD{0}\maroonD{21}\text{ milliliters}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare using $>,<,$ or $=$.**

$9$  liters [[☃ dropdown 1]] $90$ milliliters  

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
To compare, we need the numbers to have the same units.  

Let's convert $\blueD9$ liters to milliliters.

Now, we can compare.

$\blueD{9{,}000} \text{ mL}> {90} \text{ mL}$
